results.label: Standard — gaming channel with full data
results.output_summary: Generated a full report with metrics table showing 14% view growth and 26% subscriber growth MoM. Performance Highlights identifies the Reddit pickup of the 'Developer Explains' video as a viral driver, notes that the 'shame/failure' narrative format outperforms standard reviews. Areas for Attention notes that removing the Reddit-driven outlier shows organic growth is more modest. Recommendations: (1) produce more developer story content monthly, (2) engage the subreddit community directly, (3) hold publishing at 5/month, (4) test a 'why X game failed' series, (5) monitor whether Reddit-driven viewers convert to subscribers.

A1.note: All four sections present with correctly formatted metrics table.

A2.note: All 5 recommendations are numbered and specific enough to schedule.

A3.note: Identifies that 'failure/post-mortem' narrative format drives engagement beyond just view count.

results.label: Minimal — small fitness channel
results.output_summary: Generated a proportionally shorter report for a small channel. Metrics table presents the data without comparison period (noted as unavailable). Highlights the strong performance of the no-equipment workout as the channel's discovery engine. Areas for Attention notes the small sample size limits trend analysis. Recommendations: (1) create more no-equipment full-body workouts, (2) begin tracking month-over-month to enable trend analysis, (3) test a pull-up bar affiliate since the review performed well.

A1.note: All four sections present. Report is appropriately shorter due to limited data.

A2.note: Recommendations are specific and actionable despite limited input data.

A3.note: Identifies that the no-equipment format lowers the barrier to entry, explaining its overperformance relative to equipment-focused content.

results.label: European context — Dutch cycling channel
results.output_summary: Full report with MoM comparison showing 26% view growth and 23% subscriber growth. Highlights the Netherlands infrastructure video's viral spread via cycling advocacy organization. Notes that European cultural and infrastructure content outperforms pure gear content. Recommendations: (1) pitch more content to cycling advocacy organizations for amplification, (2) prioritize infrastructure and culture videos over gear reviews, (3) hold at 4 videos/month, (4) explore a EuroVelo series given the route video's performance, (5) test German and Dutch subtitles given audience geography.

A1.note: All sections present with correct MoM comparison table.

A2.note: Recommendations include European-specific actions like subtitles and cycling advocacy outreach.

A3.note: Identifies that the Netherlands video succeeded because it framed infrastructure as a cultural identity story, not just a policy explainer.
